M04-1-title.title: M04 Meat System
M04-1-title.studentContent:
Meat System

🎬 MeatProfile Visual & Audio Style

HOW it looks & sounds — How to shoot and edit your video
M04-1-title.presenterContent:
Meat System

🎬 MeatProfile Visual & Audio Style

HOW it looks & sounds — How to shoot and edit your video
M04-1-title.notes: The Bone layer tells us "how to tell the story," the Soul layer tells us "why it works," and the Meat layer tells us "how to shoot it."

M04-1-review.title: Bone-Meat-Soul Three-Layer Architecture
M04-1-review.studentContent:
🦴 Bone

HOW to tell

Skeleton + Strategy

"How to tell the story"

👻 Soul

WHY it works

SoulProfile 5 Fields

"Why it works"

🥩 Meat

HOW it looks & sounds

MeatProfile 11 Fields

"How to shoot it"

💡 Core Principle: The Meat layer "serves" Bone and Soul. Don't choose a visual style just because it "looks good" — ask "does this style support the structure and emotion?"
M04-1-review.presenterContent:
🦴 Bone

HOW to tell

Skeleton + Strategy

"How to tell the story"

👻 Soul

WHY it works

SoulProfile 5 Fields

"Why it works"

🥩 Meat

HOW it looks & sounds

MeatProfile 11 Fields

"How to shoot it"

💡 Core Principle: The Meat layer "serves" Bone and Soul. Don't choose a visual style just because it "looks good" — ask "does this style support the structure and emotion?"
M04-1-review.notes: Emphasize that Meat is the execution layer and must serve Bone and Soul.

M04-1-11fields.title: MeatProfile 11 Fields Overview
M04-1-11fields.studentContent:
📐 Format Constraints (3)

Define the "base format" of the video

• talent_framing — On-Camera Presence

• duration_range — Duration Range

• aspect_ratio — Aspect Ratio

🎨 Audiovisual Style (4)

Define the "audio-visual expression"

• voice_type — Voice Type

• shot_grammar — Shot Grammar

• camera_movement — Camera Movement

• subtitle_form — Subtitle Style

🎬 Pacing & Editing (2)

Control the "rhythm and feel"

• pacing — Pacing Control

• editing_pattern — Editing Pattern

✨ Enhancement (2)

Add "visual and audio enhancements"

• screen_elements — Screen Elements

• audio_guidance — Audio Guidance

⚠️ Tip: You don't need to memorize all 11 fields — the key is understanding that these dimensions exist!
M04-1-11fields.presenterContent:
📐 Format Constraints (3)

Define the "base format" of the video

• talent_framing — On-Camera Presence

• duration_range — Duration Range

• aspect_ratio — Aspect Ratio

🎨 Audiovisual Style (4)

Define the "audio-visual expression"

• voice_type — Voice Type

• shot_grammar — Shot Grammar

• camera_movement — Camera Movement

• subtitle_form — Subtitle Style

🎬 Pacing & Editing (2)

Control the "rhythm and feel"

• pacing — Pacing Control

• editing_pattern — Editing Pattern

✨ Enhancement (2)

Add "visual and audio enhancements"

• screen_elements — Screen Elements

• audio_guidance — Audio Guidance

⚠️ Tip: You don't need to memorize all 11 fields — the key is understanding that these dimensions exist!
M04-1-11fields.notes: Give students the big picture so they know what's coming. Emphasize that memorization is not required.

M04-2-title.title: Format Constraint Fields
M04-2-title.studentContent:
Format Constraint Fields

📐 talent_framing · duration_range · aspect_ratio

Define the "base format" of the video — these should be determined first
M04-2-title.presenterContent:
Format Constraint Fields

📐 talent_framing · duration_range · aspect_ratio

Define the "base format" of the video — these should be determined first
M04-2-title.notes: This group of fields defines the base format of the video and should be determined first.

M04-2-talent.title: talent_framing — On-Camera Presence
M04-2-talent.studentContent:
🎭 talent_framing

Determines who the "main character" of the video is

OptionDescriptionUse Case
product_onlyProduct-only showcaseProduct close-ups, ASMR unboxing
hands_demoHands-only demonstrationTutorials, application demos
body_no_faceBody visible, no faceOutfit showcase, body comparison
talking_headOn-camera host presentationProduct recommendations, reviews
full_bodyFull body on cameraFashion, fitness, lifestyle vlog
skitShort skit / scenarioComedy sketches, plot twists

Beauty & Skincare Use Cases

product_onlyProduct close-ups, ingredient lists, texture showcase, ASMR unboxing
hands_demoApplication demos, makeup process, usage tutorials
body_no_faceOutfit showcase, body lotion application, body comparison
talking_headCreator product recommendations, review sharing, educational content
full_bodyFull outfit showcase, fitness routines, lifestyle vlogs
skitPlot twists, comedy sketches, brand advertisements

🎭 talent_framing

M04-2-talent.notes: talent_framing determines the video's main character. Now includes 6 options: product_only, hands_demo, body_no_face, talking_head, full_body, skit.

M04-2-duration.title: duration_range — Duration Range
M04-2-duration.studentContent:
⏱️ duration_range

Defines the target duration range for the video

Contains 3 sub-fields:

• min_seconds: Minimum duration (sec) — below this, information can't be fully delivered

• max_seconds: Maximum duration (sec) — above this, completion rate drops significantly

• target_seconds: Target duration (sec) — ideal length balancing info density and completion rate

Recommended Duration by Platform:

• TikTok growth phase: 15-30 seconds

• TikTok standard: 30-60 seconds

• Xiaohongshu/RED product seeding: 30-90 seconds

• Bilibili in-depth reviews: 3-10 minutes

Beauty Content Duration Reference

{min:15, max:30, target:20}Fast-paced product seeding, single product showcase, before/after
{min:30, max:60, target:45}Detailed reviews, usage tutorials, ingredient breakdowns
{min:60, max:180, target:120}In-depth reviews, multi-product comparisons, full makeup looks

M04-2-duration.notes: Duration range now contains 3 sub-fields: min_seconds, max_seconds, target_seconds.

M04-2-ratio.title: aspect_ratio — Aspect Ratio
M04-2-ratio.studentContent:
📐 aspect_ratio

The width-to-height ratio of the frame, determining the video's "shape"

RatioNamePlatform / Use Case
9:16VerticalTikTok, Kuaishou, Xiaohongshu/RED (mainstream)
16:9HorizontalYouTube, Bilibili, TV commercials
1:1SquareInstagram, WeChat Moments ads

Aspect Ratio Selection for Beauty Content

9:16 Vertical (Most Common)

• Best mobile viewing experience
• Ideal for on-camera talent, product showcase
• TikTok / Xiaohongshu/RED mainstream format

16:9 Horizontal

• Ideal for multi-product comparisons
• In-depth reviews, tutorial content
• Bilibili / YouTube mainstream format
M04-2-ratio.presenterContent:
📐 aspect_ratio

The width-to-height ratio of the frame, determining the video's "shape"

RatioNamePlatform / Use Case
9:16VerticalTikTok, Kuaishou, Xiaohongshu/RED (mainstream)
16:9HorizontalYouTube, Bilibili, TV commercials
1:1SquareInstagram, WeChat Moments ads

• Bilibili / YouTube mainstream format
M04-2-ratio.notes: Aspect ratio is primarily determined by the platform.

M04-3-title.title: Audiovisual Style Fields
M04-3-title.studentContent:
Audiovisual Style Fields

🎨 voice · shot · camera · subtitle

Define the "audio-visual expression" of the video
M04-3-title.presenterContent:
Audiovisual Style Fields

🎨 voice · shot · camera · subtitle

Define the "audio-visual expression" of the video
M04-3-title.notes: This group of fields defines the audio-visual expression of the video.

M04-3-voice.title: voice_type — Voice Type
M04-3-voice.studentContent:
🎤 voice_type

The voice / voiceover approach for the video

TypeDescriptionUse Case
humanReal human voiceover / live audioProduct recommendations, reviews, knowledge sharing
ai_ttsAI text-to-speechBulk content, no facial expression needed
noneNo voiceProduct showcase, mood/ambiance videos

Voice Selection for Beauty Content

human — Real Human Voiceover

Creator product recommendations, review sharing — needs that "human touch"

ai_tts — AI Voiceover

Brand official videos, bulk matrix content

none — No Voice

Premium brand TVCs, ambiance-driven videos
M04-3-voice.presenterContent:
🎤 voice_type

The voice / voiceover approach for the video

TypeDescriptionUse Case
humanReal human voiceover / live audioProduct recommendations, reviews, knowledge sharing
ai_ttsAI text-to-speechBulk content, no facial expression needed
noneNo voiceProduct showcase, mood/ambiance videos

Voice Selection for Beauty Content

human — Real Human Voiceover

Creator product recommendations, review sharing — needs that "human touch"

ai_tts — AI Voiceover

Brand official videos, bulk matrix content

none — No Voice

Premium brand TVCs, ambiance-driven videos
M04-3-voice.notes: Voice type affects the "human touch" of the video.

M04-3-shot.title: shot_grammar — Shot Grammar
M04-3-shot.studentContent:
📷 shot_grammar

Defines per-section shot count constraints and composition rules

section_constraints — Per-Section Shot Constraints:

• min_shots: Minimum shots for the section

• max_shots: Maximum shots for the section

• min_duration_ratio: Minimum duration ratio for the section

rules — Composition Rules

Common Composition Rules

• Rule of Thirds
• Golden Ratio
• Center Symmetry

Note: Shot sizes (ECU/CU/MS/FS) are specific choices made during production, not part of the shot_grammar template definition.
M04-3-shot.presenterContent:
📷 shot_grammar

Defines per-section shot count constraints and composition rules

section_constraints — Per-Section Shot Constraints:

• min_shots: Minimum shots for the section

• max_shots: Maximum shots for the section

• min_duration_ratio: Minimum duration ratio for the section

rules — Composition Rules

Common Composition Rules

• Rule of Thirds
• Golden Ratio
• Center Symmetry

Note: Shot sizes (ECU/CU/MS/FS) are specific choices made during production, not part of the shot_grammar template definition.
M04-3-shot.notes: shot_grammar defines per-section shot constraints and composition rules. Shot sizes are production decisions, not template parameters.

M04-3-camera.title: camera_movement — Camera Movement
M04-3-camera.studentContent:
🎥 camera_movement

How the camera moves, affecting the video's sense of "motion"

TypeDescriptionEffect
staticStationary, no movementStable, professional, focused
pushPush in, toward subjectEmphasis, urgency
pullPull back, reveal full viewSpaciousness, reveal
panHorizontal sweepReveal space, multiple products
tiltVertical sweep up/downShow vertical subjects (e.g. full outfit)
trackFollow the subjectDynamic, lifestyle feel

Camera Movement for Beauty Content

static — Stationary

On-camera narration, tutorial demos — keeps the audience focused on the content

push — Push In

Emphasize product details, create tension

pull — Pull Back

Reveal full scene, create sense of spaciousness

pan — Horizontal Pan

Multi-product showcase, vanity table scenes

tilt — Vertical Sweep

Full outfit reveal, tall product showcase

track — Follow Subject

Lifestyle vlogs, dynamic movement shots

M04-3-subtitle.title: subtitle_form — Subtitle Style
M04-3-subtitle.studentContent:
📝 subtitle_form

The presentation style and format of subtitles/captions

TypeDescriptionUse Case
punchlinePunchy quote, keyword emphasisProduct seeding, emotional content
stepsStep-by-step, numberedTutorials, process walkthroughs
labelSmall annotations, supplementaryProduct names, ingredient labels
data_cardData visualization cardResults showcase, comparisons
minimalMinimal/none, visual priorityPremium brands, ambiance videos

Subtitle Selection for Beauty Content

punchline — Punchy Quote Style

"This lipstick is THE killer shade!"

steps — Step-by-Step

"Step1: Cleanse → Step2: Serum → Step3: Moisturizer"

label — Small Annotations

Product names, ingredient concentrations, small supplementary text

data_card — Data Card

"28-Day Test | Wrinkles -23% | Elasticity +18%"

minimal — Minimal

Premium brand ambiance videos, visual-first approach

M04-4-title.title: Pacing & Editing Fields
M04-4-title.studentContent:
Pacing & Editing Fields

🎬 pacing · editing_pattern

Control the "rhythm and editing style" — just 2 fields
M04-4-title.presenterContent:
Pacing & Editing Fields

🎬 pacing · editing_pattern

Control the "rhythm and editing style" — just 2 fields
M04-4-title.notes: This group controls the overall rhythm and editing style of the video.

M04-4-pacing.title: pacing — Pacing Control
M04-4-pacing.studentContent:
⏩ pacing

Overall rhythm level + per-shot duration constraints

LevelDescriptionUse Case
lowLong takes, immersiveStory narrative, brand tone, ASMR
mediumBalanced rhythm, moderate cutsSafe choice for most scenarios
highFast cuts, high info densityFlash sales, rapid product seeding

shot_duration — Per-Shot Duration Constraints

Sub-fields:

• min: Minimum seconds per shot

• max: Maximum seconds per shot

• avg: Average seconds — core editing reference

Duration Examples

• high pacing: avg 1.5-2s
• medium pacing: avg 2-4s
• low pacing: avg 4-6s
M04-4-pacing.presenterContent:
⏩ pacing

Overall rhythm level + per-shot duration constraints

LevelDescriptionUse Case
lowLong takes, immersiveStory narrative, brand tone, ASMR
mediumBalanced rhythm, moderate cutsSafe choice for most scenarios
highFast cuts, high info densityFlash sales, rapid product seeding

shot_duration — Per-Shot Duration Constraints

Sub-fields:

• min: Minimum seconds per shot

• max: Maximum seconds per shot

• avg: Average seconds — core editing reference

Duration Examples

• high pacing: avg 1.5-2s
• medium pacing: avg 2-4s
• low pacing: avg 4-6s
M04-4-pacing.notes: Pacing now uses low/medium/high levels with shot_duration sub-fields (min/max/avg).

M04-4-editing.title: editing_pattern — Editing Pattern
M04-4-editing.studentContent:
✂️ editing_pattern

Editing style description + transition list

Contains 2 sub-fields:

• style: Text description of editing style
e.g. "rapid jump cuts + beat-synced" / "steady narrative editing"

• transitions: Allowed transition types list

transitions — Transition Types

TypeDescription
hard_cutHard cut — direct switch, strong rhythm
dissolveDissolve — gradual blend, soft and natural
effectsEffects transition — zoom, spin, flash, use sparingly
M04-4-editing.presenterContent:
✂️ editing_pattern

Editing style description + transition list

Contains 2 sub-fields:

• style: Text description of editing style
e.g. "rapid jump cuts + beat-synced" / "steady narrative editing"

• transitions: Allowed transition types list

transitions — Transition Types

TypeDescription
hard_cutHard cut — direct switch, strong rhythm
dissolveDissolve — gradual blend, soft and natural
effectsEffects transition — zoom, spin, flash, use sparingly
M04-4-editing.notes: editing_pattern now has 2 sub-fields: style (text description) and transitions (allowed types list).

M04-5-title.title: Enhancement Fields
M04-5-title.studentContent:
Enhancement Fields

✨ screen_elements · audio_guidance

Add "visual and audio enhancements" — the icing on the cake
M04-5-title.presenterContent:
Enhancement Fields

✨ screen_elements · audio_guidance

Add "visual and audio enhancements" — the icing on the cake
M04-5-title.notes: This group defines visual overlays and audio configuration. Split from the previous 'Pacing & Editing' group to form the 4th independent group.

M04-5-screen.title: screen_elements — Screen Elements
M04-5-screen.studentContent:
✨ screen_elements

On-screen overlay elements and usage rules

allowed_types — 6 Element Types:

• Text overlay: Bold emphasis text for key selling points

• emoji: Emoji decorations for approachability

• data_callout: Data visualization annotations

• arrow: Arrows/indicators to guide attention

• sticker: Stickers/icons for decoration

• broll_insert: B-roll footage inserts

Rule Sub-fields

hook_requiredWhether HOOK section must have screen elements
cta_requiredWhether CTA section must have screen elements
max_text_lengthMax characters per text overlay
rulesAdditional usage rules list

Why only HOOK and CTA required? These are the video's "entrance" and "exit" — screen elements have the most direct impact on conversion.

M04-5-screen.notes: screen_elements now includes 6 types and rule sub-fields including hook_required, cta_required, max_text_length, and rules.

M04-5-audio.title: audio_guidance — Audio Guidance
M04-5-audio.studentContent:
🎵 audio_guidance

BGM style, SFX density, and usage guidelines

Contains 3 sub-fields:

• bgm_style: upbeat / emotional / minimal / none

• sfx_density: high / medium / low / none

• sfx_guidelines: Additional SFX usage guidelines list

bgm_style — BGM Style

upbeat — Energetic

Fast-paced product seeding, promos

emotional — Sentimental

Brand stories, testimonials

minimal — Subtle Background

Doesn't steal attention, ideal for talking head

none — No BGM

ASMR, pure product showcase

sfx_guidelines examples: "HOOK must have attention sound" / "product close-up gets ding SFX"
M04-5-audio.presenterContent:
🎵 audio_guidance

BGM style, SFX density, and usage guidelines

Contains 3 sub-fields:

• bgm_style: upbeat / emotional / minimal / none

• sfx_density: high / medium / low / none

• sfx_guidelines: Additional SFX usage guidelines list

bgm_style — BGM Style

upbeat — Energetic

Fast-paced product seeding, promos

emotional — Sentimental

Brand stories, testimonials

minimal — Subtle Background

Doesn't steal attention, ideal for talking head

none — No BGM

ASMR, pure product showcase

sfx_guidelines examples: "HOOK must have attention sound" / "product close-up gets ding SFX"
M04-5-audio.notes: audio_guidance now has 3 sub-fields: bgm_style (with minimal replacing chill), sfx_density, and sfx_guidelines.

M04-6-boundary.title: What Meat Profile Does NOT Include
M04-6-boundary.studentContent:
Excluded ItemWhy ExcludedWhen Decided
Styling (makeup, hair, wardrobe)Depends on specific talent and product; same Meat Profile with different products = different stylingWhen generating script
Filming location (indoor/outdoor, set)Depends on actual filming resources and product use scenariosWhen generating script
Product info (name, selling points, price)Business data, not visual style definitionWhen generating script
💡 Key Distinction: Meat Profile defines "what style to shoot in." Styling, location, and product info are "what to shoot" — the former is a reusable template parameter, the latter is per-execution input.
M04-6-boundary.presenterContent:
Excluded ItemWhy ExcludedWhen Decided
Styling (makeup, hair, wardrobe)Depends on specific talent and product; same Meat Profile with different products = different stylingWhen generating script
Filming location (indoor/outdoor, set)Depends on actual filming resources and product use scenariosWhen generating script
Product info (name, selling points, price)Business data, not visual style definitionWhen generating script
💡 Key Distinction: Meat Profile defines "what style to shoot in." Styling, location, and product info are "what to shoot" — the former is a reusable template parameter, the latter is per-execution input.
M04-6-boundary.notes: Helps students understand the boundaries of Meat Profile.

M04-6-quiz.title: Knowledge Check
M04-6-quiz.studentContent: Which of the 4 Meat Profile groups contains pacing and editing_pattern?
M04-6-quiz.presenterContent: Which of the 4 Meat Profile groups contains pacing and editing_pattern?
M04-6-quiz.question: Which of the 4 Meat Profile groups contains pacing and editing_pattern?
M04-6-quiz.explanation: Correct answer is C. The Pacing & Editing (Rhythm) group contains pacing and editing_pattern. The pacing level enum values are low / medium / high.
M04-6-quiz.notes: Tests whether students understand the 4-group structure.

M04-7-title.studentContent:
Comprehensive Case Study

🎯 Analyzing a Complete Video with MeatProfile

Comprehensive Case Study

M04-7-case.title: Case Study: Beauty Creator Product Recommendation Video
M04-7-case.studentContent:
📹 Video Description

Content: Creator on-camera recommending a serum

Platform: TikTok

Duration: Approx. 45 seconds

Style: Lively, personable, engaging

📐 Format Constraints

talent_framingtalking_head
duration_range{min:30, max:60, target:45}
aspect_ratio9:16

🎨 Audiovisual Style

voice_typehuman
shot_grammarsection_constraints + rules
camera_movementstatic
subtitle_formpunchline

🎬 Pacing & Editing

pacing{level:medium, shot_duration:{avg:3}}
editing_pattern{style:"jump cut+hard cut", transitions:[hard_cut]}

✨ Enhancement

screen_elements{types:[text overlay, emoji], hook_required:true}
audio_guidance{bgm:upbeat, sfx:medium}

M04-7-cheatsheet.title: MeatProfile 11-Field Cheat Sheet
M04-7-cheatsheet.studentContent:
GroupFieldOptionsDescription
📐 Format Constraints (3)talent_framingproduct_only / hands_demo / body_no_face / talking_head / full_body / skitOn-Camera Presence
duration_rangemin_seconds / max_seconds / target_secondsDuration Range
aspect_ratio9:16 / 16:9 / 1:1Aspect Ratio
🎨 Audiovisual Style (4)voice_typehuman / ai_tts / noneVoice Type
shot_grammarsection_constraints + rulesShot Grammar
camera_movementstatic / push / pull / pan / tilt / trackCamera Movement
subtitle_formpunchline / steps / label / data_card / minimalSubtitle Style
🎬 Pacing & Editing (2)pacinglevel(low/medium/high) + shot_duration(min/max/avg)Pacing Control
editing_patternstyle + transitions(hard_cut/dissolve/effects)Editing Pattern
✨ Enhancement (2)screen_elements6 types + hook_required / cta_required / rulesScreen Elements
audio_guidancebgm_style(upbeat/emotional/minimal/none) + sfx_guidelinesAudio Guidance
💡 Remember: You don't need to memorize all 11 fields — the key is understanding that these dimensions exist. In practice, you can always refer to this cheat sheet!
M04-7-cheatsheet.presenterContent:
GroupFieldOptionsDescription
📐 Format Constraints (3)talent_framingproduct_only / hands_demo / body_no_face / talking_head / full_body / skitOn-Camera Presence
duration_rangemin_seconds / max_seconds / target_secondsDuration Range
aspect_ratio9:16 / 16:9 / 1:1Aspect Ratio
🎨 Audiovisual Style (4)voice_typehuman / ai_tts / noneVoice Type
shot_grammarsection_constraints + rulesShot Grammar
camera_movementstatic / push / pull / pan / tilt / trackCamera Movement
subtitle_formpunchline / steps / label / data_card / minimalSubtitle Style
🎬 Pacing & Editing (2)pacinglevel(low/medium/high) + shot_duration(min/max/avg)Pacing Control
editing_patternstyle + transitions(hard_cut/dissolve/effects)Editing Pattern
✨ Enhancement (2)screen_elements6 types + hook_required / cta_required / rulesScreen Elements
audio_guidancebgm_style(upbeat/emotional/minimal/none) + sfx_guidelinesAudio Guidance
💡 Remember: You don't need to memorize all 11 fields — the key is understanding that these dimensions exist. In practice, you can always refer to this cheat sheet!
M04-7-cheatsheet.notes: Complete MeatProfile cheat sheet with correct 4-group structure (3+4+2+2).

M04-7-summary.title: M04 Summary: Complete Bone-Meat-Soul Three-Layer Picture
M04-7-summary.studentContent:
Complete Video = Bone (How to Tell) x Soul (Why It Works) x Meat (How to Shoot)

🦴 Bone

Skeleton: Video structural framework

Strategy: L0 / L1 / L2 strategy

Answers "HOW to tell"

👻 Soul

SoulProfile: 5 Fields

target_audience / trigger_scenario
reward_type / conflict_type / emotion_arc

Answers "WHY it works"

🥩 Meat

MeatProfile: 11 Fields

Format Constraints (3) + Audiovisual Style (4) + Pacing & Editing (2) + Enhancement (2)

Answers "HOW it looks & sounds"

🎯 Determination Order: First Soul (the spirit), then Bone (the structure), and finally Meat (the audio-visuals)
🚀 Next Step: Now that you've learned the three-layer architecture, tomorrow we'll learn how to use AI to help us complete these analyses more efficiently!
